growth_records.strain: Sodalis glossinidius (tsetse secondary endosymbiont; genome-scale model iLF517)

up.method: in silico FBA model constraint (iLF517), NOT measured; chosen from H. pylori microaerophile model
in silico FBA model constraint used in the earlier iEB458 model (E. coli-derived aerobic value; deemed unrealistic here)
Experimentally shown to be the preferred carbon source: equimolar GlcNAc gave ~2x greater endpoint OD than D-glucose (P<0.05); growth measured as endpoint OD650 only, no uptake rate reported

mfa.note: in silico FBA output of iLF517, NOT measured
in silico FBA output of iLF517, NOT measured; qualitatively matches experimental result that GlcNAc > glucose
in silico FBA output of iLF517, NOT measured

model_biomass_yields_gDW_per_mmol.note: in silico FBA only, not experimental

growth_records.cite: Hall RJ et al. A Tale of Three Species: Adaptation of Sodalis glossinidius to Tsetse Biology, Wigglesworthia Metabolism, and Host Diet. mBio (2019). DOI:10.1128/mBio.02106-18. [PMC6315101]
growth_records.snippet: 17 mM GlcNAc, 17 mM trehalose, 17 mM L-serine, 17 mM L-arginine, 4 mM L-proline, 17 mM L-glutamic acid monosodium salt hydrate, 17 mM L-aspartate, 4 mM nicotinamide, 9 mM alpha-ketoglutarate, 9 mM fumaric acid, and 0.4 mM thiamine monophosp
growth_records.notes: GEM/FBA paper (model iLF517: 517 genes, 703 metabolites, 638 reactions). No experimental uptake or secretion RATES are reported; experimental growth is endpoint OD650 only (SGM11 reaches ~0.7; Fig 2A). Cell count calibration: OD650 0.28 ~ 3.8e8 cells/ml (SEM 9.7e7) by flow cytometry. All quantitative fluxes here are in silico: O2 uptake constraint 12 mmol gDW-1 h-1 (iLF517) vs 20 (iEB458); biomass yields 0.30/0.35/0.32 gDW per mmol for glucose/GlcNAc/mannitol. A high-glutamate variant of SGM11 uses 5x L-glutamate = 85 mM (base described as ~18 mM). Culture is oxygen-limited/microaerophilic (organism is O2-sensitive), 25C, batch flask, 500 rpm stir. Experimental carbon-source ranking (endpoint OD): GlcNAc > D-glucose ~ mannitol > others; ptsG pseudogenised, GlcNAc taken up via NagE. Thiamine/TMP is an experimentally confirmed auxotrophy (growth abolished without TMP). No mu, doubling time, pH, or dilution rate given.
